heading: Federal broadband program coordination
label: 950bb–6 Federal broadband program coordination
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Agriculture Improvement Act of 2018, and not as part of the Rural Electrification Act of 1936 which comprises this chapter.
Definition of “Secretary”“Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture, see , set out as a note under .section 2 of Pub. L. 115–334section 9001 of this title

children.heading: Consultation between USDA and NTIA
children.label: a Consultation between USDA and NTIA
children.text: The Secretary shall consult with the Assistant Secretary to assist in the verification of eligibility of the broadband loan and grant programs of the Department of Agriculture. In providing assistance under the preceding sentence, the Assistant Secretary shall make available the broadband assessment and mapping capabilities of the National Telecommunications and Information Administration.

children.heading: Consultation between USDA and FCC
children.label: b Consultation between USDA and FCC

children.heading: By USDA
children.label: 1 By USDA
children.text: The Secretary shall consult with the Commission before providing broadband assistance for a project to serve an area with respect to which another entity is receiving Connect America Fund or Mobility Fund support under the Federal universal service support mechanisms established under .section 254 of title 47

children.heading: By FCC
children.label: 2 By FCC
children.text: The Commission shall consult with the Secretary before offering or providing Connect America Fund or Mobility Fund support under the Federal universal service support mechanisms established under to serve an area with respect to which another entity has received broadband assistance under a loan or grant program of the Department of Agriculture.section 254 of title 47
Promote high-quality broadband service that meets the long-term needs of rural residents and businesses, by evaluating the broadband service needs in rural areas for each decade through 2050.

children.label: A
children.text: The real-world performance of such technologies, including data rates, latency, data usage restrictions, and other aspects of service quality, as defined by the Commission.

children.label: B
children.text: The suitability of each such technology for residential, agricultural, educational, healthcare, commercial, and industrial purposes in rural areas.

children.label: C
children.text: The cost to deploy and support such technologies in several rural geographies.

children.label: D
children.text: The costs associated with online platforms, specifically the resulting constraints on rural network bandwidth.

children.label: A
children.text: Harmonize broadband notification and reporting requirements and develop common verification procedures across all federally supported broadband programs.

children.label: B
children.text: Consolidate and utilize the existing broadband service data.

children.label: i
children.text: support under a broadband assistance program of the Department of Agriculture; or

children.label: ii
children.text: Connect America Fund or Mobility Fund support under the Federal universal service support mechanisms established under .section 254 of title 47

children.label: D
children.text: Leverage support technologies and services from online platforms for providers of broadband service in rural areas.

children.heading: Assistant Secretary
children.label: 1 Assistant Secretary
children.text: The term “Assistant Secretary” means the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Communications and Information.

children.heading: Commission
children.label: 2 Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Federal Communications Commission.

children.heading: Rural area
children.label: 3 Rural area
children.text: The term “rural area” has the meaning given the term in .section 950bb(b)(3) of this title
